title: The Valve Wire Weekly - 2026-04-04
description: Weekly podcast covering transcatheter valve technology developments for the week ending 2026-04-04.
show_notes_html:
The Valve Wire Weekly - 2026-04-04

Duration: 23:42 | Hosts: E. Nolan Beckett, MD & Claire Marchand, MBA

Summary

Welcome back to The Valve Wire Weekly. I'm Nolan Beckett, and this is episode two — and what a week to be covering. ACC Scientific Sessions wrapped up in Chicago, and the structural heart world delivered some genuinely practice-changing data. It really did, Nolan. I'm Claire Marchand, and I have to say, the contrast between the clinical headlines and what's happening in the stock market this week is striking. We've got landmark trial results, new FDA approvals, and yet some of these valve stocks are getting absolutely hammered. We've got a lot to cover. The PRO-TAVI trial published in The Lancet — that's our headliner — telling us we may not need to do PCI before TAVR. Cerebral embolic protection devices took a beating in not one but two studies. And a fascinating Japanese registry that says frailty, not age, is what really matters in nonagenarians.

description: Weekly podcast covering transcatheter valve technology developments for the week ending 2026-03-25.

Summary

Welcome to The Valve Wire Weekly, the podcast covering the latest in transcatheter valve technology. I'm Dr. Nolan Beckett, your host and structural heart specialist. This is our very first episode, and what a week to launch — we've got historic FDA approvals, concerning durability data, and some fascinating new research. Thanks Nolan, and welcome everyone! I'm Claire Marchand, bringing you the market and industry perspective. This week really had everything — from JenaValve's groundbreaking approval for aortic regurgitation to some sobering long-term data on mitral repair that's got the whole field talking.
